mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales reflect robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive RSAFS surprises are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds restrictive levels, elevated discount rates and borrowing costs dominate equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and rise in payroll employment signals acute labor market tightness that boosts wage income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression predicting 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery, a rebound in industrial production captures renewed manufacturing momentum and inventory rebuilding, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO surprises coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of recovery episodes identified by GDPC1 troughs.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening construction activity and household formation, foreshadowing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative HOUST surprises do not correlate with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When elevated nominal rates coincide with high inflation, the resulting restrictive real rate environment and margin compression jointly reduce equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS > 4% and CPIAUCSL > 300 does not produce negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor demand and impending consumption slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within three months.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production reflects stronger manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports near-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO surprises do not lead to positive 5-day S&P 500 returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 2-year rolling window.
